title: Bridge Essay: Physiology of long pranayamic breathing: neural respiratory elements may provide

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Breath/Soul** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Breath and soul — pranayama, breath-brain coupling, respiratory-brain interaction, cardiorespiratory entrainment, vagal pathways of consciousness.

**Physiology of long pranayamic breathing: neural respiratory elements may provide amechanism that explains how slow deep breathing shifts the autonomic nervous system.** — Med Hypotheses. PMID: 16624497. DOI: 10.1016/j.mehy.2006.02.042. Pranayamic breathing, defined as amanipulation of breath movement, has been shown to contribute to aphysiologic response characterized by the presence of decreased oxygen consumption, decreased heart rate, and decreased blood pressure, as well as increased theta wave amplitude in EEG recordings, increased parasympathetic activity accompanied by the experience of alertness and reinvigoration.
